DmSendBinary

Sends binary data from a development PC over an existing connection to an Xbox.

DMHRAPI DmSendBinary(
  PDM_CONNECTION pConnection, 
  LPCVOID pv, 
  DWORD cb
  );

Parameters

pConnection
Pointer to a DM_CONNECTION structure that contains information about the connection.
pv
Pointer to the binary data to send.
cb
Number of bytes to send.

Return Values

Returns XBDM_NOERR if successful, an error value otherwise.

Remarks

Programs on a development PC can use the DmSendBinary function transmit binary data to an XBE running on an Xbox. To do so, programs typically call the DmSendCommand function to tell the XBE that the binary data is about to be transmitted. Next, programs call DmSendBinary and transmit the data. The DmReceiveStatusResponse function must be called immediately after DmSendBinary or the debug channel gets out of sync and behaves as if the session was disconnected.

To receive data, a program on a development PC typically calls DmSendCommand to tell the XBE to transmit data. It then calls the DmReceiveBinary function to retrieve the data.

To communicate binary data, a program running on the Xbox must have a command processor function. It registers the command processor function by invoking DmRegisterCommandProcessor. At runtime, the command processor function handles commands sent to it from DmSendCommand. When it gets a command informing it that binary data is about to be transmitted, it reads the data from a buffer that is accessible in the PDMN_CMDCONT structure the command processor receives through its parameter list.

The command processor also is used to transmit data to the development PC. When it gets a command from DmSendCommand indicating that it is time to transmit data, the command processor function fills the buffer in its PDMN_CMDCONT structure. The data is sent to the development PC when the command processor function returns.

Requirements

Header: Declared in XboxDbg.h
Import Library: Use XboxDbg.lib
